TSLA: Tesla's Europe Registrations Rise 10% In February Despite Sales Drops In Key Markets. Tesla Inc. saw a 10% year-over-year increase in its European registrations in February, totaling 17,425 units, despite sales drops in some key markets like the UK and Netherlands. While February showed a surge, Tesla's year-to-date registrations for January and February are slightly below the previous year's figures. Meanwhile, Chinese competitor BYD reported a significant 165% increase in European registrations in January.

Tesla, Inc. $TSLA is Vinva Investment Management Ltd's 8th Largest Position. Vinva Investment Management Ltd increased its stake in Tesla by 25.9% in Q3, making it the fund's 8th largest holding. Despite this, insider selling has been significant, with directors James R. Murdoch and Kimbal Musk selling large blocks of shares. Wall Street analysts present a mixed view, resulting in a consensus "Hold" rating, and while Tesla beat Q4 EPS estimates, revenue was down year-over-year.

Roundhill TSLA WeeklyPay ETF To Go Ex-Dividend On March 9th, 2026 With 0.20504 USD Dividend Per Share. The Roundhill TSLA WeeklyPay ETF (TSLW.US) is set to go ex-dividend on March 9th, 2026, with a dividend payment of 0.20504 USD per share. Shareholders on record by this date will receive the cash dividend on March 10th, 2026. The article also provides general information on the differences and implications of cash dividends versus stock dividends.

Surprise! America Has Chosen Its Favorite Auto Brand -- and It's Not Ford, General Motors, or Tesla!. Hyundai Motor Company has been identified as America's favorite auto brand for the 17th consecutive year, according to Brand Keys' 2026 Customer Loyalty Engagement Index. This is attributed to its strong value proposition, reliable warranty, and high quality ratings, consistently undercutting competitors on price. Despite the enduring popularity of Ford's F-Series trucks and the visibility of brands like Tesla and General Motors, Hyundai has maintained its lead in customer loyalty and engagement.

Tesla Rival BYD Applies For Permit To Allow Canada Imports After Tariff Agreement. BYD Co. Ltd. has applied for permits to import its vehicles into Canada, registering factories in Shenzhen and Xi'an as potential exporters. This move follows a reduced tariff agreement between Ottawa and Beijing, allowing over 49,000 Chinese EVs (potentially expanding to 70,000) to enter Canada at a 6.1% tariff. While BYD sees strong overseas sales growth, its domestic sales have recently been surpassed by Geely Automobile Holdings Ltd.

Tesla Sued by Texas Worker Claiming Assault by Austin Shooter. Tesla is being sued for over $1 million by Lillian Mendoza Brady, a former employee, who claims she was assaulted by Ndiaga Diagne, the individual police have identified as the Austin mass shooter. The lawsuit alleges Tesla failed to ensure workplace safety and negligently supervised Diagne, who reportedly had "known aggressive tendencies" and had assaulted Brady during a company-sanctioned prayer break in December.

Ford makes big shift into energy storage to keep battery plants busy. Ford is entering the battery energy storage system business, called Ford Energy, to repurpose a Kentucky factory originally planned for EV battery production, due to slower-than-expected electric vehicle sales. The company will produce large-scale energy storage units for utility companies and data centers, facing competition from established players like Tesla. Ford believes its manufacturing expertise and licensing of CATL technology give it an advantage in this new venture.

Rivian Wants R2 To Accomplish Something Ford, GM Couldn't: Here's The Record It's Chasing. Rivian aims for its R2 electric vehicle to achieve 20,000 deliveries within six months of launch, a feat that only Tesla's Model Y has accomplished faster. This ambitious goal sets the R2 apart from other EV launches by Ford and GM, highlighting Rivian's efforts to rapidly scale production and capture market share with a more affordable EV option. Rivian is retooling its Normal, Illinois factory to meet this demand and will unveil official pricing and the full R2 lineup soon at SXSW.
TGT: Argus and Mizuho Lift Lowe's (LOW) Targets, See Growth and Market Share Gains. Argus and Mizuho have raised their price targets for Lowe's (LOW) following a Q4 earnings beat, signaling confidence in the company's long-term growth and market share gains. Argus increased its target to $288 with a Buy rating, while Mizuho analyst David Bellinger lifted his target to $294 with an Outperform rating, viewing a recent selloff as an overreaction to the fiscal 2026 outlook. Both firms expect Lowe's, a home improvement retailer, to perform well despite potential market fluctuations.

Target Corp. Experiences Evaluation Revision Amid Mixed Market Signals and Performance Trends. Target Corp. has undergone an evaluation revision due to mixed market signals and performance trends. While the stock has declined 7.11% over the past year, it boasts a 16.41% year-to-date return, outperforming the S&P 500. Technical indicators show a blend of bullish and bearish signals, highlighting the company's resilience despite long-term challenges.

Munich Reinsurance Co Stock Corp in Munich Buys Shares of 37,859 Target Corporation $TGT. Munich Reinsurance Co Stock Corp in Munich has acquired a new stake of 37,859 shares in Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) during the third quarter, valued at approximately $3.396 million. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their holdings in Target, and hedge funds collectively own 79.73% of the company's stock. Target's recent earnings report showed $2.44 EPS, surpassing estimates, and analysts have issued mixed ratings, with an average target price of $115.76.

Neo Ivy Capital Management Sells 26,632 Shares of Walmart Inc. $WMT. Neo Ivy Capital Management significantly reduced its stake in Walmart Inc. by 82.1% in the third quarter, selling 26,632 shares and retaining 5,820 shares valued at approximately $600,000. While corporate insiders have been net sellers, other large institutions like Vanguard and Norges Bank have increased their positions, with institutional investors now owning about 26.76% of Walmart stock. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price near $135.76, reflecting positive sentiment around Walmart's tech and AI strategies despite some negative sentiment regarding regulatory settlements and potential tariff risks.

Dynamic Technology Lab Private Ltd Has $2.62 Million Holdings in Lowe's Companies, Inc. $LOW. Dynamic Technology Lab Private Ltd reduced its stake in Lowe's Companies by 38.3% during the 3rd quarter, now owning 10,433 shares valued at $2.62 million, making it 0.4% of their portfolio. Despite this reduction and a CEO share sale, Lowe's recently beat quarterly earnings expectations and holds a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price of $291. Several other hedge funds, however, increased their holdings in Lowe's during the same period.

Barclays Lifts CF Industries Price Forecast Amid Geopolitical Tensions. Barclays has increased its price target for CF Industries to $120, citing expected higher nitrogen prices driven by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The article highlights CF Industries' strong market position, robust profitability, and sound financial health, while also noting recent insider share sales as a point of caution. Despite a historical revenue decline and market normalization risks, the outlook for CF Industries remains predominantly favorable due to strategic growth initiatives and its resilience against sector benchmarks.

Munich Reinsurance Co Stock Corp in Munich Takes $14.92 Million Position in Kimberly-Clark Corporation $KMB. Munich Reinsurance Co Stock Corp in Munich has initiated a new position in Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NASDAQ:KMB), purchasing 119,986 shares valued at approximately $14.92 million in the third quarter. Kimberly-Clark recently raised its quarterly dividend to $1.28, implying a 4.9% yield, and reported quarterly EPS of $1.86, beating the consensus estimate. The company's stock currently has an average "Hold" rating from analysts with a consensus price target of $119.00.

Jim Cramer on Target: "It's Just Way Too Cheap If the Company Can Maintain a Sustained Recovery". Jim Cramer expressed his belief in Target's new management, citing a better-than-expected quarter and analyst upgrades. He highlighted that Target's stock is currently trading at a significantly lower earnings multiple compared to competitors like Walmart and Costco, suggesting it could be a "steal" if the company sustains its recovery under CEO Michael Fiddelke. While Cramer is encouraged, he emphasized the need for positive same-store sales, continued margin improvement, and legitimate earnings growth for full confidence.

Analysts Are Bullish on Top Consumer Goods Stocks: Procter & Gamble (PG), Target (TGT). RBC Capital analysts have issued bullish ratings for Procter & Gamble (PG) and Target (TGT). Nik Modi maintained a Buy rating on Procter & Gamble with a $172.00 price target, while Steven Shemesh maintained a Buy rating on Target with a $130.00 price target. Despite these optimistic individual ratings, the broader analyst consensus for Target is a Hold.

Analysts Are Bullish on Top Consumer Goods Stocks: Procter & Gamble (PG), Target (TGT). Two analysts have issued bullish sentiments on Procter & Gamble (PG) and Target (TGT) within the Consumer Goods sector. RBC Capital maintained a Buy rating on Procter & Gamble with a $172 price target, while RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on Target with a $130 price target. The article details the reasoning behind these ratings and the analysts' track records.

Kroger names chief data and AI officer. Kroger has appointed Milen Mahadevan as its first chief data and AI officer, emphasizing the grocer's commitment to accelerating AI across its operations. Mahadevan, who also leads Kroger's retail analytics subsidiary 84.51˚, will focus on developing "agentic" AI capabilities to enhance both customer experience and business productivity. This move aligns with broader retail trends where companies like Target and Albertsons are also increasing AI investments to boost efficiency and improve customer interactions.

Starbucks (SBUX) Stock Rises on Turnaround Signs: Latest Price, Q1 2026 Earnings and Outlook as of March 2026. Starbucks (SBUX) stock increased by 0.30% to $98.99 on March 6, 2026, driven by signs of operational recovery. The company reported a 6% year-over-year revenue increase to $9.9 billion for Q1 fiscal 2026, with global comparable store sales up 4%. CEO Brian Niccol's "Back to Starbucks" strategy, focusing on premium experiences, efficiency, and customer engagement, aims to sustain this positive momentum amid ongoing challenges in global markets like China.

Dimensional Fund Advisors LP Has $238.66 Million Stock Holdings in Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. Dimensional Fund Advisors LP has increased its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 1.0%, now owning 2,820,720 shares valued at approximately $238.66 million. This move is part of a broader trend of institutional investment, with firms like Norges Bank, Corient Private Wealth LLC, and Invesco Ltd. also adding significant positions, bringing total institutional ownership to 72.29%. Despite missing EPS estimates in its latest quarter, Starbucks exceeded revenue expectations and holds an average "Moderate Buy" analyst rating with a target price of $104.22.

The Corporate Miscreants Driving the Affordability Crisis. A new report from the Institute for Policy Studies (IPS) highlights how 20 major corporations, the "Low Wage 20," contribute to the affordability crisis by paying workers wages so low they rely on public assistance. These companies, including Amazon and Starbucks, have seen CEO pay soar while median worker pay has declined after inflation. The article suggests policy changes like raising the minimum wage, bolstering labor rights, and implementing "bad business fees" to address these disparities.

DA Davidson Sees Starbucks (SBUX) Turnaround Progress but Initiates at Neutral. DA Davidson initiated coverage of Starbucks (SBUX) with a Neutral rating and a $97 price target, acknowledging the company's turnaround efforts for sustainable growth but noting uncertainty regarding the pace of same-store sales improvement and margin visibility. Simultaneously, Starbucks announced plans to open a new corporate office in Nashville, Tennessee, later this year to support its North American supply chain and expand its presence, offering relocation opportunities to Seattle-based employees and creating new roles.

Starbucks to close store in Seattle's University District. Starbucks plans to close its University District store in Seattle on April 5th, following a three-month strike. This closure is part of a larger $1 billion turnaround plan the coffee company is implementing. The announcement was made by Egan Ward for the Puget Sound Business Journal.

DA Davidson Sees Starbucks (SBUX) Turnaround Progress but Initiates at Neutral. DA Davidson has initiated coverage of Starbucks (SBUX) with a Neutral rating and a $97 price target, acknowledging progress in the company's turnaround efforts. However, the firm expresses uncertainty regarding the pace of same-store sales improvement and visibility on regaining historical flow-through margins. Separately, Starbucks is expanding its corporate presence by opening a new office in Nashville, Tennessee, to manage its North American supply chain and support growth in the central US, South, and Northeast.
DIS: MediaCo Holding Inc. Appoints René Santaella as Chief Growth & Innovation Officer, Effective March 9, 2026. MediaCo Holding Inc. has appointed René Santaella to the newly created role of Chief Growth & Innovation Officer, effective March 9, 2026. In his new role, Santaella will be responsible for leading the strategy and execution of MediaCo's Supply + Growth Engines across TV, Audio, and Digital & Streaming. He previously served as Chief Operating Officer of MediaCo since October 2024 and has extensive experience in digital, streaming operations, and advertising monetization from his prior roles at Estrella Media Inc., Sony Pictures Television, and Disney.

The Walt Disney Company $DIS Shares Sold by Rathbones Group PLC. Girl group i-dle's Yuqi chosen as one of Chinese music giant's 'Women of the Year'. Yuqi of the K-pop girl group i-dle has been recognized as one of Tencent Music's Women of the Year in Music 2026 for her contributions to the Chinese music market, particularly with her solo single "Motivation." She also received several accolades on Tencent Music's Korean chart and recently sang the theme song for Shanghai Disneyland's 10-year anniversary. Meanwhile, i-dle made history as the first K-pop girl group to perform at the Taipei Dome.

Orion Porfolio Solutions LLC Sells 9,176 Shares of The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Orion Portfolio Solutions LLC has reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company (DIS) by 9.9%, selling 9,176 shares and retaining 83,182 shares valued at approximately $9.52 million. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for DIS, with an average target price of $135.80, while the stock currently trades near $101.57. Despite recent positive earnings and operational momentum in parks and cruises, the company faces mixed sentiment due to leadership transition concerns, liquidity management, and a reputation-related safety allegation.

WEBTOON Entertainment Inc. launches Mickey and Friends F1 adventure on digital platform. WEBTOON Entertainment Inc. has released a new digital series featuring Mickey and Friends entering the world of Formula 1 racing. The story involves Mickey and his friends assisting a struggling racing team, bringing popular Disney characters to the high-stakes F1 environment exclusively on WEBTOON. This initiative aims to blend animation with motorsport to attract a global online audience.

The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) Receives Average Rating of "Moderate Buy" from Brokerages. The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) has received an average "Moderate Buy" rating from 24 brokerages, with an average one-year target price of $135.80. The company recently reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings and revenue, and has strengthened its liquidity with new credit lines. However, investor sentiment faces pressure from a management transition and concerns over promotions potentially diluting near-term margins.
NKE: Dow Jones Movers: IBM Leads, Sherwin-Williams Drags as Analysts Clash on Salesforce. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 2.95% last week due to tariff anxieties, macro uncertainty, and a VIX spike. IBM and Microsoft led gains as investors rotated into software stocks, while Sherwin-Williams, Caterpillar, and Nike dragged, impacted by consumer staples sell-offs and tariff exposure. Salesforce saw conflicting analyst ratings, reflecting debate on its AI pivot.

Nike Restructuring Under Elliott Hill Tests Turnaround And Profitability Hopes. Nike is undergoing a significant restructuring under CEO Elliott Hill, expecting $300 million in pre-tax charges related to employee severance, as part of its "Win Now" turnaround program. This initiative aims to realign costs, invest in growth, and enhance competitive positioning amidst challenges like sluggish sales and margin pressure. Investors will be observing how effectively Nike can convert these operational changes into sustained performance and profitability, particularly in its sport performance categories and wholesale strategy.
